$comment: Reference TEMPLATE. By itself it does nothing -- scripts/*/register-claude-code-mcp.*
merges the entries you name into ~/.claude.json (USER scope).
GRAPHIFY -> register ONE user-scope `graphify` entry (defined below), cwd-relative. It is
stdio + a static per-cwd graph.json, so one definition serves each repo its OWN graph -- no
per-repo entry, and with a single definition there is no user-vs-project precedence conflict
to have. Workstation: the `graphify` uv entry below. Server (coding.vm, Docker): swap the
command for the cwd wrapper -- "graphify": { "command": "graphify-mcp" }, from
infra/mcp-servers/bin/graphify-mcp on PATH. The retired per-repo `graphify-docker` (2026-07-20)
used a HARDCODED mount, which is what made one repo's graph answer for every repo.
Details: skills/mcp-servers-setup/SKILL.md -> Graphify.
OMNIGRAPH is the opposite -- genuinely PER-REPO (project scope, pinned by OMNIGRAPH_GRAPH_ID).
Keep user scope EMPTY of `omnigraph`: a 2026-07-17 incident had a user-scope `omnigraph` win
over the project one (an unapproved .mcp.json loads no project servers at all, which looks
identical). See CLAUDE.md 'MCP config precedence'. Serena and playwright are genuinely
repo-agnostic and fine in user scope.
A PROJECT server (omnigraph) must ALSO be approved in <repo>/.claude/settings.local.json ->
enabledMcpjsonServers (untracked; a tracked .mcp.json cannot approve itself). An unapproved
server is skipped silently. Verify graphify wiring -- single user-scope entry, NO project
graphify entry, graph built -- with:
bash scripts/linux/check-graphify-scope.sh --fix
